2605.03598.title: Unifying Dynamical Systems and Graph Theory to Mechanistically Understand Computation in Neural Networks
2605.03598.paper_summary: Understanding how biological and artificial neural networks implement computation from connectivity is a central problem in neuroscience and machine learning. In neural systems, structural and functional connectivity are known to diverge, motivating approaches that move beyond direct connections alone. Here, we show that the spatial and temporal function of recurrent neural networks (RNNs) trained on hierarchically modular tasks can be recovered by modelling the network as a graph and analysing the multi-hop pathways between input and output units. In particular, decomposing these pathways by hop length reveals how the network temporally routes information. This perspective reframes regularisation: if function is implemented through multi-hop communication, then standard penalties such as L1 regularisation, which act only on individual weights, constrain single-hop structure rather than the multi-hop pathways that support computation. Motivated by this view, we introduce resolvent-RNNs (R-RNNs), which constrain multi-hop pathways and thereby induce temporal sparsity beyond that achieved by standard L1 regularisation. Compared with L1 regularisation, R-RNNs achieve improved performance by inducing temporal sparsity that matches the task structure, even when the task signal is sparse. Moreover, R-RNNs exhibit stronger sparsity-function alignment, reflected in their increased robustness under strong regularisation. Together, our results identify multi-hop communication as a key principle linking structure to function in recurrent networks, and suggest that sparsity should be defined over functional pathways rather than individual parameters.
2605.03598.paper_summary_zh: 理解生物和人工神經網絡如何從連接性實現計算是神經科學和機器學習中的一個核心問題。<lb/>在神經系統中，結構和功能連接性已知會出現偏差，這促使了超越僅僅直接連接的研究方法。<lb/>在此，我們展示了在分層模塊任務上訓練的遞歸神經網絡（RNN）的空間和時間功能可以通過將網絡建模為圖形並分析輸入和輸出單元之間的多跳路徑來恢復。<lb/>特別是，通過跳躍長度分解這些路徑揭示了網絡如何在時間上路由信息。<lb/>這一視角重新框定了正則化：如果功能是通過多跳通信來實現的，那麼像L1正則化這樣僅作用於單個權重的標準懲罰，限制的是單跳結構，而不是支持計算的多跳路徑。<lb/>受到這一觀點的啟發，我們引入了解算子RNN（R-RNN），它限制多跳路徑，從而在超越標準L1正則化所達到的稀疏性。<lb/>與L1正則化相比，R-RNN通過誘導與任務結構匹配的時間稀疏性來提高性能，即使在任務信號稀疏的情況下也是如此。<lb/>此外，R-RNN展現出更強的稀疏性-功能對齊，這表現在它們在強正則化下的穩健性增強上。<lb/>總之，我們的結果將多跳通信確定為連接結構與功能的關鍵原則，並建議稀疏性應該在功能路徑上而不是單個參數上進行定義。
2605.03598.author: Jatin Sharma et.al.
2605.03598.authors: Jatin Sharma, Danyal Akarca, Dan F. M Goodman
